compiled_state.title: Behavioral testing standard and agent prompt reform
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context

Test-writing agents produce tests that check implementation details rather than observable behavior. The root cause is that agents test tasks (rename X to Y produces a test asserting X is gone) instead of behavior (the system works correctly using Y). Research confirms LLMs follow actual implementation rather than expected behavior when generating assertions (~25% false positive oracles, Di Grazia et al. ASE 2025). The RED test writer already has rejection criteria for change-detector tests, but the guidance exists in only one agent — fix-bug, sprint task execution, and review resolution have no equivalent. Additionally, implementation-plan does not distinguish between tasks that change behavior (requiring RED tests) and tasks that change implementation without changing behavior (requiring only GREEN validation), causing unnecessary test creation.

## Success Criteria

- All test-writing agent prompts (RED test writer, fix-bug, sprint task execution, review resolution) consume a shared behavioral testing standard with 4 sequential rules: (1) before writing — check for existing coverage, (2) what to test — Given/When/Then, one test one behavior, no internal method names, (3) how to test — execute code, assert observable outcomes, never read source files in assertions, mock only external boundaries, (4) after writing — refactoring litmus test
- Implementation-plan classifies each task's testing mode: RED (behavior changes), GREEN (implementation changes without behavior change), or UPDATE (behavior changes with existing test coverage — update existing tests); implementation-plan frames testing tasks in terms of behavior rather than mechanics
- The sprint orchestrator is updated to consume the testing_mode field from implementation-plan task output and route to the appropriate test-writing behavior per mode
- The RED test writer prompt includes an explicit no new tests needed exit path for GREEN-classified tasks and as an escape hatch when the agent determines existing coverage is sufficient
- Fix-bug integrates the RED/GREEN/UPDATE testing model: defaults to GREEN or UPDATE; RED is the exception for coverage gaps. Hypothesis validation tests are allowed but must be marked as scaffolding for later removal
- The RED test evaluator (dso:red-test-evaluator) prompt is updated to align with the shared behavioral testing standard, adding refactoring survival and suite-level value as rejection criteria

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

Create a shared behavioral testing standard as a prompt fragment (referenced by all test-writing paths) containing a 4-rule decision tree grounded in Google's unchanging test principle, Khorikov's resistance-to-refactoring pillar, and Sam Hatoum's refactoring litmus test. Update implementation-plan to emit a testing_mode field per task. Update sprint orchestrator to consume the field and route accordingly. Update fix-bug to default to GREEN/UPDATE with scaffolding support for hypothesis validation. Update RED test evaluator to align rejection criteria.

## Research Reference

See plugins/dso/docs/designs/TESTING_RESEARCH.md and plugins/dso/docs/designs/TESTING_SESSION.md for full research findings and session context.
